voice.line: Lead with patient outcomes and the cost of poor glucose control — clinical evidence first, then whole-system savings (fewer admissions, less staff time). Not a price play.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Framework-verified: 'Insulin Pumps, Continuous Glucose Monitoring, Products Contributing to the Delivery of Hybrid Closed Loop Pathways and Associated Products' (2025/S 000-4043, Medical and Surgical Consumables) — Abbott Laboratories Ltd named among 12 awarded suppliers. NOT tagged Pathology and laboratory: Abbott Laboratories Ltd / Abbott Rapid Diagnostics Ltd / Abbott Toxicology Ltd are awarded on the Lab Diagnostics/POCT framework, but those read as separate Abbott business divisions from this specific 'Abbott Diabetes Care' supplier record, whose catalogued products are diabetes-only. FreeStyle Optium Neo confirmed via NHS dm+d as a Drug Tariff item — a different route (community prescribing), not NHSSC — noted, not tagged as a separate speciality.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: added Blood and transfusion for Alinity s-series (donor blood/plasma screening -- HBsAg, anti-HCV, anti-HBc, HTLV I/II, Chagas) -- already bundled into the 'Alinity ci / h / s / m' product line but had no matching speciality tag; this family genuinely sits apart from general pathology, likely procured directly by NHS Blood and Transplant rather than the general NHS Supply Chain catalogue. Added BinaxNOW/Determine/Actim/SureStep -- the former Alere rapid-diagnostics range (Abbott acquired Alere Oct 2017), confirmed live on NHS Supply Chain under a distinct legal entity 'ABBOTT RAPID DIAGNOSTICS LTD' (separate from 'ABBOTT LABORATORIES LIMITED (BERKS)', which covers i-STAT/ARCHITECT/Alinity) -- kept as one combined 'Abbott Diagnostics' record since both are diagnostics-not-diabetes, even though NHS Supply Chain itself treats them as two legal suppliers. CONFIRMED clean separation from Abbott Diabetes Care: FreeStyle Libre is supplied via the NHS Drug Tariff (Part IXR)/community prescribing, an entirely different UK procurement route from the hospital Supply Chain catalogue used by this record -- no overlap or double-counting risk between the two supplier records. ARCHITECT is being gradually migrated to Alinity ci (both still live on NHS Supply Chain today) -- described as a managed transition, not a discontinuation, don't flag ARCHITECT as withdrawn.

alerts.text: Ansell completed acquisition of Kimberly-Clark's PPE business (Kimtech, KleenGuard brands) for $640m, closed 02/07/2024, Europe order-handling began ~March 2025. frameworks.name: NHS Supply Chain — Renal Replacement Therapies (2023/S 000-017117, to 2028)
frameworks.note: named supplier — verify lot at source

alerts.text: Re-tagged primary speciality to Anaesthesia and critical care (kept Renal and dialysis as a secondary tag) -- CE-marked indications are cytokine/bilirubin/myoglobin adsorption in critical illness and intraoperative anticoagulant removal during cardiac surgery, not renal replacement itself (it's commonly run in-line with a CRRT circuit, which is a delivery mechanism, not the clinical indication). NICE has published three Medtech Innovation Briefings -- MIB87 (sepsis), MIB217 (COVID-19 respiratory failure), MIB249 (cardiac surgery bleeding reduction) -- all note the evidence base is low-quality/small-sample/mostly observational; current sepsis evidence does not show improved mortality. Any efficacy claims should be qualified as emerging, not established.

Do NOT list DrugSorb-ATR (ticagrelor removal — FDA De Novo denied April 2025, unclear CE status) or HemoDefend-RBC/-BGA (blood-transfusion safety filters) as available products -- both are investigational/pipeline-stage only, not approved anywhere for the DrugSorb-ATR/HemoDefend line.
MERGED with a duplicate 'CytoSorbents Medical UK Ltd' record listing the same core CytoSorb cartridge under 'Renal and dialysis' only.

voice.line: The blood-purification adjunct for sepsis/critical-care nephrology — adds a therapy layer onto CRRT.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: expanded from 1 to 4 named products, merged a duplicate record, re-tagged primary speciality to critical care. Confirmed genuine entity CytoSorbents Medical UK Limited (Companies House 13992244).

The old Companies House entity SIRONA DENTAL SYSTEMS LIMITED (04893127) was dissolved 17/03/2020. Separately announced a restructuring plan targeting ~$120m savings and a $144m impairment on orthodontic/implant solutions; dividend eliminated. No divestiture of the dental imaging (Orthophos/Xios) business found -- an earlier-looking filing about 'XRAY' refers to the company's NASDAQ ticker, not an X-ray/imaging division sale.

alerts.text: MAJOR CORPORATE CHANGE: Exactech, Inc. filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in October 2024, driven by ~2,600 patient lawsuits over a packaging/oxidation defect (vacuum bags missing an oxygen barrier layer, causing polyethylene insert oxidation across 140,000+ knee, ~130,000 hip and ~1,500 ankle systems, plus the Equinoxe shoulder system) -- recall ran August 2021 through April 2024. Court confirmed a restructuring plan 15/09/2025; assets sold to a new investor group (Strategic Value Partners, Stellex Capital Management, Greywolf Capital Management). The business relaunched as ADVITA ORTHO, LLC on 03/11/2025, which now owns and distributes the Equinoxe/Vantage/Alteon/Truliant/Novation/Newton/GPS product lines. TPG Capital (the brief's assumed current owner) no longer owns the business -- a litigation trust was set up to pursue claims against TPG. UK legal entity Companies House record shows 'ADVITA ORTHO UK LIMITED' with 'previous name: EXACTECH (UK) LIMITED'. NHS Supply Chain now refers to the supplier as 'Advita Ortho (Exactech) Ltd.' Recall litigation/restructuring fallout is still live as of the most recent sources -- do not present the recall as resolved-and-behind-them without this context.
alerts.source: SEC/court filings / medtechdive.com / prnewswire.com / Companies House

voice.line: Strong shoulder franchise (Equinoxe) plus ExactechGPS navigation and predictive analytics.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: confirmed Equinoxe (shoulder) and Vantage (ankle, already listed as ExactechGPS-adjacent -- note ExactechGPS is navigation, kept as-is) live on NHS Supply Chain under supplier 'EXACTECH UK LTD'. Truliant/Alteon/InteGrip not independently confirmed on NHS Supply Chain this pass -- flagged company-site-only. Recorded the major Chapter 11 -> Advita Ortho corporate change in alerts[] -- this fundamentally changes who owns and operates the company behind these product names.

alerts.text: CORRECTION: iDose TR (previously listed as a current product) is NOT currently available in the UK/NHS -- FDA-approved and commercially launched in the US only (2024), absent from Glaukos's own UK products page, with 'international iDose TR expansion' described only as a forward-looking investor focus area. As a drug-device combination product it would additionally need a medicines marketing authorisation, a materially higher bar than the pure-device iStent line -- no evidence this has happened. iAccess (newer goniotomy device) is also not confirmed for UK availability. NICE guidance HTG432 (formerly IPG575/IPG396) covers the generic trabecular-stent-bypass procedure, not any specific Glaukos device by name, and does not cover drug-eluting implants at all.

alerts.text: CRITICAL: the original Lenus Health Ltd (Companies House SC652563) entered administration 07/01/2025 and was DISSOLVED 28/02/2026. A related company incorporated by the same CEO (Paul McGinness) two months before administration -- 'Lenus Healthcare Limited' (SC828358) -- was renamed 'Solersha Ltd' shortly after administration began; its registered office matches the current lenushealth.com contact address, but NO confirmed public source states which entity now legally operates the Lenus Health brand. The company's own newsroom has no posts after 13/12/2024, consistent with the administration timeline. Treat this as an unstable/uncertain vendor pending direct confirmation -- do not present as a stable long-term supplier without checking directly with Lenus first.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Multi-speciality review 20/07/2026: MiniMed 780G + Guardian CGM -> Diabetes/CGM. GI Genius (AI colonoscopy) -> both Endoscopy and Gastroenterology, since it's specifically a colonoscopy AI tool, not generic endoscopy. Nellcor pulse oximetry -> Patient monitoring. DBS Percept -> Neurosciences. | Renal and dialysis: confirmed on the Renal Replacement Therapies framework via its Bellco brand (CRRT/acute dialysis machines, acquired 2016). | MAJOR STRUCTURAL CHANGE 20/07/2026: Medtronic's Diabetes business (MiniMed 780G, Guardian CGM, Simplera Sync, InPen) has SPUN OFF as MiniMed Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: MMED), IPO completed 9 March 2026. Medtronic retains ~90% ownership post-IPO but this is now commercially a separate company, not a Medtronic product line — Diabetes/CGM tag removed from Medtronic and MiniMed products moved to a new 'MiniMed Group' supplier record. Verified independently via SEC 8-K/10-Q filings and press coverage, not taken on a single research pass alone. Also note: Puritan Bennett 980 and 840 ventilators (already listed) were DISCONTINUED by Medtronic in March 2024 — still in NHS service estates under existing support agreements (7-10yr), but no longer sold new. Hugo RAS robotic surgery system is confirmed LIVE at Guy's & St Thomas' NHS (first UK NHS site, not just private). Affera/Sphere-9 mapping-ablation catheter is newly launched UK PRIVATE-SECTOR-FIRST (HCA UK), not yet standard NHS. NIM nerve monitoring confirmed as a genuine ENT/head-and-neck surgery product, adding ENT as a new speciality for Medtronic. | 20/07/2026 CORRECTION: removed 'NovaSure (endometrial ablation)' -- independently confirmed this is a HOLOGIC product, not Medtronic's (Hologic's own investor/product pages, SEC 10-Ks, AND NHS Supply Chain under supplier 'HOLOGIC LTD' with live NPC codes all attribute NovaSure to Hologic; already correctly listed on Hologic UK's own record). This was my own misattribution error from an earlier batch this session -- removed the 'Women's health / maternity' speciality tag too, since NovaSure was the only product supporting it and no other genuine Medtronic women's health/maternity product exists in this record. Medtronic's actual comparable hysteroscopic-space product is TruClear (tissue removal, competes with Hologic's MyoSure) -- not currently added, flagging as a possible future addition if wanted, not added now to avoid overclaiming without its own dedicated verification pass.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Framework-verified via the downloaded NHS Supply Chain Product Matrix for 'Interventional Cardiology, Interventional Radiology and Interventional Neuroradiology, Cardiac Rhythm Management and Electrophysiology' (2021/S 000-017565, Medical Technology): Merit Medical UK Limited = Yes on Lot 1 (Interventional Cardiology/Radiology, covering Prelude/PreludeSYNC/Embosphere) and Lot 2 (Interventional Neuroradiology, covering SwiftNINJA) -> Cardiology + Neurosciences. Vascular access added from this record's own PRE-EXISTING 'frameworks' entry ('NHS Supply Chain — Syringes & Needles', £250m/3yr, from 01/07/2026, 47 suppliers) — identical wording to Cardinal Health's entry for the same framework, which reads as a templated note from a prior bulk scrape rather than hand-written prose, but the underlying award is independently plausible (same framework, same figures) and follows the same Syringes/Needles -> Vascular access convention used elsewhere. Not found independently by this round of research. DELIBERATELY NOT tagged from the CVC framework, and this is a real catch confirmed by BOTH sources: the research AND this record's own pre-existing note ('Delisted from the CVC framework') agree Merit Medical is explicitly DELISTED from 'Central Venous Catheters and Associated Products' at its 23/03/2026 start. Their actual products (arterial access sheaths, post-catheterisation compression) are cath-lab/interventional tools, a different category from the peripheral/central venous access our 'Vascular access' speciality's CVC framework covers — worth knowing the tag above comes from a different, genuinely-relevant framework (Syringes & Needles), not the CVC one. NOT TAGGED, flagged as genuine gaps: HeRO Graft (a haemodialysis access graft — 'Renal and dialysis' is the obvious clinical fit by product identity, but the Renal Replacement Therapies framework was checked and does not list Merit Medical) and SCOUT breast localisation (no NHSSC framework found under any search angle). Both left untagged rather than forced, per the same standard applied throughout this exercise.

suppliers.name: Patients Know Best
products: Single Patient Record (SPR) — core FHIR-native platform (unified patient/provider data, population health management engine, SNOMED/ICD auto-coding)

Configured use-cases on the SPR platform: Mental Health (PROMs/mood tracking), Elective Recovery (part of the national Wayfinder programme — waiting-list validation, PIFU), Remote Care (100+ device integrations), Cancer (Somerset Cancer Register integration)

alerts.text: PKB does NOT sell condition-specific products under separate names -- it is one Single Patient Record platform with configured deployments per clinical use-case. First PHR fully integrated into the NHS App (launched 14/07/2020); all hospital test results in the NHS App arrive via PKB, 20m+ results/month, live on the NHS Wales App since 2025. June 2025: nationwide GP data integration launched. Over one-third of England's Mental Health Trusts use PKB. £6m funding Jan 2025; strategic investment from DNV (Norwegian assurance/risk group) Sept 2025, amount undisclosed. Could not confirm a current total NHS trust count from primary sources.

voice.line: Patient-owned record; Hull cut missed appointments 39%. [established]

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: confirmed genuinely single-platform with configured use-cases (not separate products), added NHS App integration detail and funding history.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: removed NeuMoDx -- QIAGEN confirmed discontinuation of NeuMoDx and Dialunox, cited as a ~$20m revenue headwind in 2025 and ~$10m expected in 2026, being wound down. Added therascreen (EGFR/KRAS) companion-diagnostic PCR kits under a new Oncology and infusion tag -- these are genuinely high-stakes: a positive/negative therascreen result gates NHS prescribing eligibility for specific licensed targeted cancer drugs (EGFR-TKIs, KRAS G12C inhibitors, anti-EGFR antibodies in colorectal cancer), sitting at the pathology-oncology interface rather than routine diagnostics. NOTE: exhaustively tested the NHS Supply Chain catalogue for every QIAGEN family (Qiagen, QuantiFERON, therascreen, QIAstat, QIAamp, EZ1, digene) -- zero results for all of them, though the search tool itself was confirmed working (nitrile gloves returned 453 results, 'PCR kit' surfaced Roche/Illumina reagents). This means QIAGEN's entire range should be treated as company-site-confirmed only, NOT NHS-Supply-Chain-catalogue-confirmed -- doesn't mean these aren't used in the NHS (QuantiFERON is a well-known NHS TB-screening standard in practice), just that procurement likely runs through a separate pathology/trust-direct route not indexed in this catalogue.

alerts.text: FDA Warning Letter (06/12/2024) names RX Daytona, RX Daytona+ and RX Imola specifically for inadequate risk analysis around reagent carryover between sequential assays, following five US Class II recalls since December 2018 (an Oct 2023 incident saw total-protein reagent carryover cause falsely elevated copper results). This is a US FDA action -- no UK/MHRA equivalent finding independently confirmed. Flag before using RX-series reliability claims in UK-facing sales content.

RX series, Evidence biochip and RIQAS confirmed genuine on randox.com but company-site-only for NHS Supply Chain purposes -- Randox IS an awarded supplier on the Pathology/POCT framework (139 suppliers, not the 122 previously noted -- that figure was stale) but capital equipment/reagents beyond QC materials aren't catalogue-searchable, likely procured via direct/mini-competition routes. Recorded the FDA warning letter in alerts[]. Also worth knowing (not added as a product-data change): a 2022 NAO investigation found DHSC awarded Randox PCR-testing contracts worth up to £776.9m during COVID (2020-21), 85% without competition -- historical, not current, but well-documented if due-diligence context is ever needed.

alerts.text: SIRT (selective internal radiation therapy) is a percutaneous, catheter-based procedure performed by interventional radiologists within a hepatobiliary/liver MDT -- IR is the operating speciality, oncology the referring/MDT context. NHS England routinely commissions SIRT specifically for chemotherapy-refractory/intolerant, unresectable colorectal cancer liver metastases (per the 2014-2017 Commissioning through Evaluation review with NICE, Oct 2017); SIRT for intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma is explicitly NOT routinely commissioned. Routine-commissioning status for primary HCC specifically could not be confirmed either way.
alerts.source: NHS England specialised commissioning policy library / British Liver Trust

alerts.text: Owned since 2018 by a consortium of CDH Genetech (CDH Investments) and China Grand Pharmaceutical and Healthcare Holdings (Grand Pharma 51% / CDH 49%), delisted from the ASX 24/09/2018 -- no ownership change found since. Main competing product is TheraSphere Y-90 glass microspheres, which belongs to Boston Scientific (via its 2019 BTG acquisition), NOT Sirtex -- do not conflate the two companies' Y-90 products or their respective dosimetry software (Sirtex/MIM's SurePlan LiverY90 vs Boston Scientific/Mirada Medical's Simplicit90Y).
alerts.source: Wikipedia / PR Newswire / Boston Scientific product pages

Extended a further 6 months as of the most recent update (28/05/2026), now applying to 12 specific product codes (KFK228, KFK1298, KFK1294, KFK1302, KFK1299, KFK1295, KFK1301, FNG85008, FNG85013, FNG85007, FNG85018, FNG85019). One academic study (J Clin Microbiology 2020, COVID-era) found Sarstedt dry swabs in saline achieved lower SARS-CoV-2 RT-PCR detection than a CDC-recipe VTM -- a single study caveat, not a recall, noted for completeness only. NHS access route: Omnipod 5 has NICE Technology Appraisal TA943 (Dec 2023) recommending hybrid closed loop for eligible people with Type 1 diabetes, delivered via NHS England's 5-year national HCL rollout (from April 2024, ICB-funded) and the National Framework for Insulin Pumps, CGM and Associated Products -- not a general NHS Supply Chain catalogue purchase. NICE notes Omnipod 5 was not tested in pregnancy (avoid SmartAdjust in that group).

Live UK Field Safety Corrective Action (ref 9056196-05/20/2026-002-C, dated 27/05/2026) affects specific lots of Omnipod 5, DASH and Eros pods -- cannula tubing tear risk causing insulin under-delivery. UK patients directed to Insulet's UK support line and the MHRA Yellow Card Scheme.

alerts.text: Zero Novo Nordisk presence on the NHS Supply Chain public catalogue (searched 'Novo Nordisk' and 'NovoPen' -- no results; 'insulin pen' returns only third-party pen NEEDLES from other suppliers) -- correct, expected: insulins, GLP-1s and even the reusable pens are dispensed via NHS prescribing/community pharmacy (Drug Tariff), not procured as NHS Supply Chain devices.

Mike (Maziar) Doustdar became President & CEO 07/08/2025, succeeding Lars Fruergaard Jørgensen.

alerts.text: Axonics, Inc. was acquired by Boston Scientific, completed 15/11/2024 ($3.7bn equity value) -- no longer an independent NASDAQ company, now 'Axonics, a Boston Scientific company'. Bulkamid came via the Contura Ltd acquisition, which closed 25/02/2021 (not 2022) for up to $235m (SEC filings). Two UK Companies House entities: Axonics Modulation Technologies U.K. Limited (09622653, the SNM business) and Axonics Women's Health Limited (03145216, formerly Contura Limited, the Bulkamid business) -- same registered office, distinct SIC codes. NICE guidance: MTG50/HealthTech 554 (SNM for refractory OAB), MIB259 (SNM for faecal incontinence); IPG138/HealthTech 86 covers urethral bulking procedures generically, not Bulkamid specifically. Do not confuse with an unrelated dissolved 'Axonics Limited' (Swansea, dissolved 2012).

alerts.text: IMPORTANT NICE nuance: NICE MTG46 (Dec 2019) is a routine-adoption/cost-saving recommendation SPECIFIC TO CLUSTER HEADACHE, underpinning MedTech Funding Mandate inclusion. Migraine use is covered only by an earlier, lower-status safety clearance (not an MTG) -- do not imply NICE-mandated NHS funding for migraine use to the same degree as cluster headache. NHS Supply Chain catalogue listing was extended to 17/03/2026 with a further-extension option -- current status past that date is unconfirmed, worth re-checking. Parent electroCore, Inc. (Nasdaq: ECOR) remains independent and profitable (FY2025 net sales $32.0m, +27% YoY) -- no acquisition/ownership change. A separate consumer-wellness product, Truvaga (general stress/anxiety/sleep, no headache indication), is sold DTC by the US parent -- UK/NHS clinical channel presence unconfirmed, not added as a UK product.
alerts.source: NICE MTG46 / electrocore.gcs-web.com FY2025 results / Companies House 09432721

voice.line: No implant, no surgery — a materially easier conversation with a headache specialist than an implantable device.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: confirmed genuinely single clinical product, added the migraine-vs-cluster-headache NICE distinction and NHS Supply Chain listing expiry flag.

CORRECTION: UK availability is LIMITED, not general -- launched in England only (13/12/2024) via the NHS Specialised Services Devices Programme, restricted to select centres of excellence; first implants at UCLH. alerts.text: NICE HTG709 (formerly IPG781, Jan 2024) is CONDITIONAL, not a blanket approval: post-stroke patients WITH a tracheostomy may use it under special arrangements (governance notification, consent covering uncertainty, mandatory outcome audit); all other populations (non-tracheostomy, non-stroke) are restricted to formal research studies only. Do not present as a general NICE approval. Ownership history: Nestlé Health Science-backed 2016-2025 (PSC filings show Nestlé S.A. control until ceased 29/08/2025); now independent, backed by EQT Life Sciences/Sectoral Asset Management following a $42m Series D (March 2024). NHS deployment scale unverified beyond the company's own claim of 3,000+ commercial treatments.
alerts.source: NICE HTG709 / Companies House 06308699 PSC filings / PRNewswire Series D announcement

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: confirmed genuine single-device system (two named components), corrected the NICE guidance to reflect its conditional/population-restricted status, added the Nestlé ownership history.

UK entity Avanos Medical UK Limited (Companies House 09051011) formerly Halyard Health UK Limited (renamed Feb 2019). Do not confuse with the unrelated 'AVANOS MED LTD' (16738228, a London cafe/restaurant business, coincidental name only). NICE HTG686 (formerly IPG767) covers knee OA denervation; NICE NG59 covers low back pain facet denervation (criteria-gated, not blanket approval) -- NO dedicated NICE guidance found for sacroiliac joint denervation specifically, do not claim NICE approval for that indication.
alerts.source: avanos.investorroom.com / Companies House 09051011 / NICE HTG686 & NG59

alerts.text: Confirmed genuinely single-product (CUE1 -> CUE1+ is the same product line, not a second device). Sales of CUE1+ are CURRENTLY PAUSED while completing regulatory process updates -- confirmed independently via Parkinson's UK's Tech Guide. Not broadly NHS-prescribable -- only known NHS involvement is a 2023 pilot purchase of 10 devices at Addenbrooke's (Cambridge University Hospitals NHS Trust) for inpatient use, charitably funded, explicitly a pilot/evaluation not a national listing. 'CE-marked and MHRA-registered' is company/secondary-source claimed, not independently verified against a primary regulator database. ~$17.7m raised over six rounds including a $10m seed (2021, Amadeus Capital/Parkwalk-led).

voice.line: A wearable, not an implant — a much easier first conversation for patients hesitant about surgery.

alerts.text: Confirmed genuinely single hardware product. Received FDA approval Dec 2025 (first at-home brain-stimulation device approved for depression in the US), US launch targeted Q2 2026, prescription-only in the US -- this is a US regulatory milestone, not UK/NHS-relevant directly. UK/EU: CE-marked Class IIa (specific certificate numbers not independently verified), sold direct-to-consumer, no prescription required privately. Not on general NHS prescription -- a 2026 NHS Innovation Accelerator fellow with pilot activity across 5 named NHS trusts (Leicestershire Partnership, Northamptonshire Healthcare, Norfolk and Suffolk, Rotherham Doncaster and South Humber, West London) plus NHS Practitioner Health -- local pilots/QI projects, not national commissioning. NICE HTG382 (tDCS for depression, general) recommends use only 'with special arrangements for clinical governance, consent and audit or research' -- do not overstate as NICE-approved. ~$13m raised Oct 2025, plus One Mind Accelerator investment Feb 2026 tied to US expansion.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: confirmed genuinely single-product, flagged the Dec 2025 FDA approval and clarified the limited (pilot-only) UK NHS status.

alerts.text: CRITICAL: P14 Medical Limited entered Creditors' Voluntary Liquidation 26/08/2023 and remains in liquidation (most recent liquidator's statement filed 16/10/2025) -- NOT dissolved but NOT currently trading. No live website found (both p14medical.co.uk and .com fail to resolve). Its distribution agreement with Avanos ended 24/05/2020 when Avanos began selling direct -- so even before liquidation, the one confirmed product relationship was already over 3 years stale. Still appears as a named supplier on the NHS Supply Chain 'Neuromodulation Devices and Associated Products Framework Agreement' (live from 18/03/2024, AFTER the liquidation date) -- this is an unresolved catalogue anomaly, not evidence of current trading; do not treat the NHS Supply Chain listing as proof this company is active. Do NOT present this record as a current supplier without direct confirmation.
alerts.source: Companies House 10911187 (insolvency filing) / vlex.co.uk High Court judgment

alerts.text: Voluntary global recall of RelayPro Thoracic Stent-Graft System announced 01/07/2026 (NBS models 32-46mm, ~7,000 units sold May 2024-March 2026 across 43 countries). Reports of the delivery system failing to separate from the deployed graft, associated with three deaths. Shipments outside Japan suspended since March 2026.

The legal/trading entity is Vascutek Limited (Companies House SC079773, Inchinnan, Renfrewshire -- name history: Vascutek 1982-1996, Sulzer Vascutek 1996-2002 under then-owner Sulzer, reverted to Vascutek following Terumo's 2002 acquisition). A separate 'Terumo Aortic Limited' (SC593256) exists on Companies House but is registered DORMANT -- do not cite it as the trading entity. NHS Supply Chain lists these products under supplier 'VASCUTEK LTD', not 'Terumo Aortic'. No canonical 'vascular surgery' speciality tag exists in this taxonomy -- added Theatres/surgical as the closest secondary fit for the open surgical graft lines (Gelweave, Thoraflex Hybrid); note clinically this sits in vascular surgery and cardiothoracic surgery (Thoraflex Hybrid specifically), not general cardiology.
alerts.source: Companies House SC079773 & SC593256 / terumoaortic.com / NHS Supply Chain catalogue

suppliers.note: Terumo's aortic stent-graft division, formed from the 2018 merger of Vascutek and Bolton Medical; UK manufacturing base in Glasgow. Kept as a separate supplier record from Terumo UK (interventional/vascular access) and Terumo BCT (apheresis) as it is a distinct manufacturing and commercial entity.

voice.line: TEVAR stent-graft supplier -- RelayPro under an active global recall as of July 2026, worth knowing before referencing it in a meeting.

alerts.text: MERGED with a duplicate 'Pelican Healthcare Limited' record. Confirmed the same commercial entity three independent ways: (1) pelicanhealthcare.co.uk now 301-redirects to eakinostomy.com/gb/ with an on-page notice confirming the brand absorption; (2) Pelican Healthcare Limited (Companies House 02648823) has been 75%+ owned by Eakin Healthcare Group Limited since 01/04/2019; (3) NHS Supply Chain lists eakin-branded products (e.g. Eakin Freeseal) with supplier name shown literally as 'PELICAN HEALTHCARE LIMITED'. CORRECTION to an earlier session note: this ostomy-brand duplication is unrelated to Armstrong Medical, a separate Eakin Healthcare Group company in respiratory care (acquired ~2020/21) -- do not conflate the two.
alerts.source: Companies House 02648823 PSC filing / eakinostomy.com/gb / NHS Supply Chain catalogue

suppliers.note: Part of Eakin Healthcare Group Ltd (Comber, Co Down, family-owned since 1974); its stoma/wound manufacturing is done through its Cardiff subsidiary Pelican Healthcare Ltd, which is the entity that appears as the NHS Supply Chain supplier of record ('PELICAN HEALTHCARE LIMITED', 26 confirmed live SKUs). Sister company to Armstrong Medical (respiratory/anaesthesia, also Eakin-owned) -- kept as a separate record since the product ranges don't overlap at all. NOT related to Eakin Surgical Ltd (Cardiff), which was sold to Vernacare in 2026 and is unrelated to this business going forward.

voice.line: Family-owned since 1974, genuine UK manufacturing depth in stoma seals/pouches and wound-drainage management.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: new record created to properly separate Eakin's own ostomy/wound range from Armstrong Medical (respiratory), which it had been incorrectly bundled with under the name 'Armstrong Medical (Eakin)'. Sourced from eakinostomy.com and confirmed live on NHS Supply Chain under supplier PELICAN HEALTHCARE LIMITED.20/07/2026: merged the duplicate Pelican Healthcare Limited record, added 4 named product ranges (ModaVi, ModaVi Convex, Pelican Platinum, Pelican Select), all NHS-Supply-Chain-confirmed.

suppliers.name: SC Johnson Professional (Deb)
products: Deb / Deb Cutan (hand wash, alcohol hand rub, moisturiser)
Deb Stoko (hand hygiene kits, dispenser systems)
Alcare / Alcare Plus / BactoShield / Cal Stat (ex-STERIS surgical skin-prep range, acquired 2016)
suppliers.note: A current division of the privately-held S.C. Johnson & Son, Inc. (Windex, Pledge, Scrubbing Bubbles) -- re-entered the professional/institutional hygiene market by acquiring Deb Group (hand hygiene/skin care) in 2015, then bought STERIS's Hand Hygiene and Surface Disinfection business in 2016. Kept as a SEPARATE record from Diversey -- no common ownership since 2010 despite the historical 'JohnsonDiversey' name overlap (2002-2010). NHS Supply Chain confirms Deb products under supplier 'SC JOHNSON T/A DEB LIMITED RIPLEY', entirely distinct from 'DIVERSEY LTD'.

voice.line: Deb is one of the most extensively NHS-Supply-Chain-listed hand hygiene ranges (100+ confirmed SKUs).

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: new record created to correct the earlier 'Diversey / SC Johnson Professional' bundle -- these are two independently-owned companies (Diversey now Solenis-owned, this business S.C. Johnson family-owned) with no common ownership since 2010. Deb/Cutan confirmed extensively NHS-Supply-Chain-confirmed (100+ SKUs). Alcare/BactoShield/Cal Stat/Kindest Kare/Soft 'N Sure (ex-STERIS, 2016 acquisition) confirmed genuine but NOT found on NHS Supply Chain -- only US-market distributor sourcing found, flagged as unverified for UK/NHS use, not added with confidence.

suppliers.name: Crest Medical Ltd
products: Stericlens (sterile saline wound cleansing solution, 100ml/240ml — NHS-Supply-Chain-confirmed)
Irripod (20ml sterile saline irrigation pod — NHS-Supply-Chain-listed but current SKU shown SUSPENDED)

alerts.text: New record split from CD Medical Ltd -- Stericlens and Irripod moved to Crest Medical Ltd (Irripod acquisition confirmed 2023 via Crest's own site; Stericlens transition date unconfirmed). Confirmed via NHS Supply Chain catalogue (both brands listed under this supplier) and crestmedical.co.uk / irripod.co.uk.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: new record created after research confirmed Stericlens/Irripod moved here from CD Medical Ltd.
